title: Receipt
description: A payment receipt issued after a transaction is captured. Receipts are immutable after issuance. The schema_version field aligns with the platform's payment_receipt.v1 schema. The hash field is the SHA-256 digest of the canonical receipt payload using stable JSON serialization (lexicographic key order, no whitespace, UTF-8). Additional governance and provenance fields are included in the full receipt payload.

schema_version.description: Schema version identifier. Always 'payment_receipt.v1' in V1.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp when the receipt was issued.

transaction_id.description: The transaction this receipt was issued for.

authorization_id.description: Internal authorization ID that preceded the transaction.

agent_id.description: The agent that initiated this transaction.

agent_name.description: Display name of the agent at time of issuance.

merchant_id.description: The merchant receiving the payment, if applicable.

amount.description: Amount in minor units (cents for USD).

rail.description: Payment rail used for this transaction.

purpose.description: Human-readable purpose string declared at authorization time.

purpose_tag.description: Machine-readable purpose tag for policy filtering.

decision_record_id.description: Decision record bound to this payment via attach-links. Links the AI system's decision lineage to this financial action.

policy_id.description: The payment policy that authorized this transaction.

policy_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the policy at evaluation time, for audit reproducibility.

artifact_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the AI artifact associated with this transaction.

certificate_id.description: CertifiedData certificate ID linked to this payment.

external_reference.description: External session or transaction reference (e.g. Stripe checkout session ID).

status.description: Payment status at time of receipt issuance (submitted or succeeded).

sha256_hash.description: SHA-256 digest of the canonical receipt payload. Included in the capture response for independent verification. Use this to verify receipt integrity without calling the verify endpoint.

ed25519_sig.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 signature over the canonical receipt payload. Included in the capture response. The signing key can be discovered at /.well-known/certifieddata.json.

signing_key_id.description: ID of the signing key used. Resolves to a public key in the platform key registry.

livemode.description: True for live mode receipts, false for sandbox.

onchain.description: Onchain settlement fields. Present only for crypto rails.

examples.purpose: Certified dataset purchase for training run
